Arkansas · State Licensing Reference

Speech-Language Pathologist
Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP) Guide

Issued by the Arkansas Board of Examiners in Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology under the ABESPA. Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Telepractice Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$100
$75 application + $25 license fee
Renewal
$75
Every year
Processing Time
2 to 4 weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
1 year
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Arkansas Board of Examiners in Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology

Mailing Address

101 E. Capitol Ave., Suite 211, Little Rock, AR 72201

Physical Address

101 E. Capitol Ave., Suite 211, Little Rock, AR 72201

Phone & Fax

Phone: (501) 682-9180

Fax: (501) 682-9181

Email

abespa@arkansas.gov

Credential Categories

Arkansas SLP License Types

Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist

The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Arkansas.

Provisional SLP License

Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship under supervision.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Arkansas Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP)

Earn a Master's Degree in SLP

Complete a master's degree from a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program.

Pass the Praxis Examination

Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology (test code 5331, score 162).

Complete Clinical Fellowship

Complete 36 weeks of full-time supervised clinical practice (1,260 hours).

Submit Application to the Board

Submit application with $75 fee plus $25 license fee. Include transcripts, Praxis scores, and CF verification.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official graduate transcriptsFrom a CAA-accredited program.
  • Praxis exam score reportScore of 162 or higher on test 5331.
  • Clinical Fellowship verificationCF completion documentation.
  • Background checkCriminal background check as required.
Telepractice

Arkansas Telepractice Rules for SLPs

Telehealth Practice

Arkansas permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Clinical Fellowship & Provisional Rules

Arkansas issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Arkansas is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Arkansas through a compact privilege.

Frequently Asked Questions

Arkansas SLP FAQ

How much does an Arkansas SLP license cost?

The total initial cost is approximately $100. Annual renewal is $75. Note that Arkansas requires annual rather than biennial renewal.

Is Arkansas part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

Yes. Arkansas has enacted ASLP-IC legislation.

Does Arkansas allow telepractice for SLPs?

Yes. Arkansas permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and compact privilege holders.
